title: Twins at the Zoo
story_html: My brother and I are twins. We are helpful at home. Today we visit the zoo with dad. We see a big reptile. It has rough scales. It looks brave and strong. At school, I like recess. We play a fun game outside. Then we have art class. We use scissors to cut paper. It is important to be careful. After school, we eat a meal together. Mom cooks fresh vegetables. For dessert, we have something sweet. It tastes like candy.

helpful.def: Willing and ready to help others.

brave.def: Not afraid to do something difficult.

meal.def: The food eaten at one time, like breakfast or dinner.

fresh.def: Newly made or grown, not old.

scales.def: The small hard pieces that cover a reptile's body.

recess.def: A short break between lessons at school.

reptile.def: An animal with scales that is usually cold-blooded.

scissors.def: A tool with two blades, used for cutting.

twins.def: Two children born to the same mother at the same time.

questions.qtext: What are the writer and his brother?
questions.explain: The story says, "My brother and I are twins."

questions.qtext: What animal do they see at the zoo?
questions.explain: The story says, "We see a big reptile."

options: Small hard pieces on a reptile's body
questions.explain: Scales are the small hard pieces that cover a reptile's body.

questions.qtext: What do they use in art class?
questions.explain: The story says, "We use scissors to cut paper."

questions.qtext: "It is important to be careful." What does important mean?
questions.explain: Important means having great meaning or value, so being careful matters a lot.
